moduleTitle: Electromagnetic Theory: Eddy Current Generation and Impedance

lessons.title: Generation of Eddy Currents by an AC Field
objectives: Explain how an alternating current in a test coil generates eddy currents in a conductive part
Describe the conditions required for eddy current generation
Sketch the basic coil-to-part energy transfer process
cp105Topics: 2.1.1 Generation of eddy currents by means of an AC field

lessons.title: Fields Created by Eddy Currents and Impedance Changes
objectives: Describe the secondary magnetic field produced by induced eddy currents
Explain how the secondary field changes coil impedance
Define impedance as the combination of resistance and inductive reactance
cp105Topics: 2.1.2 Effect of fields created by eddy currents (impedance changes)

lessons.title: Effect of Impedance Change on Instrumentation
objectives: Trace how a coil impedance change becomes a usable instrument signal
Identify what an eddy current instrument actually measures
Relate signal amplitude and phase to changes in the test part
cp105Topics: 2.1.3 Effect of change of impedance on instrumentation

lessons.title: Reading the Impedance Plane
objectives: Interpret basic impedance-plane displays for common test variables
Distinguish liftoff, conductivity, and flaw response directions on the impedance plane
Apply impedance concepts from lessons 2.1.1-2.1.3 to practical signal recognition
cp105Topics: 2.1 Eddy current theory (impedance-plane integration and review)
2.1.2 Effect of fields created by eddy currents (impedance changes) - applied
2.1.3 Effect of change of impedance on instrumentation - applied
